---
title: 'Installation instructions' 
author: 'Fabio Molo and Gorka Fraga Gonzalez'
date: last-modified

---

To follow along in the hands-on part of the workshop, please install ```Quarto``` on your computer.  

## Installation using R and RStudio

Recent versions of RStudio have built-in support for ```Quarto```. Therefore, the simplest way to use it is to install recent versions of R (we recommend version 4.4) and RStudio by following the instructions at <https://posit.co/download/rstudio-desktop/>.

To render documents in PDF format, ```Quarto``` needs to be able to access ```LaTeX```. If you do not already have ```LaTeX``` installed, you can install the light-weight version ```TinyTex``` by typing the following in your **Terminal**:

```bash
quarto install tinytex
```

Additional instructions regarding ````LaTeX```, if necessary, can be found here: <https://quarto.org/docs/output-formats/pdf-basics.html>


## Stand-alone installation (optional)

In the workshop we will use R and RStudio for demonstration, but ```Quarto``` can also be used with Python instead of R and with VS Code, Jupyter Notebooks or other development environments instead of RStudio. If this is of interest to you, the following page is a good starting point: <https://quarto.org/docs/get-started/hello/vscode.html>

Please also make sure to have a working version of LaTeX installed (as above). 


## Alternative: working from the R Posit cloud 
If you do not want to install anything in your computer you can also use a cloud version of R and RStudio. Sign up at the **Posit Cloud** <https://login.posit.cloud> and:

-  register with your email address and give a password;
-  select the "Free Cloud" subscription option.
-  Once logged in, go to your workspace and click on `New Project` (top right corner), then choose `New RStudio Project`. 


## Installing additional R packages
We will use the following additional R packages in the workshop. You can install them on the spot or beforehand by typing the following code in the **R console**: 
```r
install.packages(c('quarto', 'dplyr', 'ggplot2'))
```
